chatbot-vs-agent.instruction: Explain the difference between a chatbot and an AI agent to a business stakeholder. Include a concrete example of each.
chatbot-vs-agent.placeholder: A chatbot is... An agent is...
chatbot-vs-agent.successMessage: That distinction is the foundation of everything in this course.

the-agent-loop.instruction: Describe each step of the agent loop from receiving a task to completing it. Include the think-act-observe cycle.
the-agent-loop.successMessage: Think, Act, Observe, Repeat. That's the heartbeat of every agent.

the-system-prompt.instruction: Write a system prompt for an AI agent that handles customer refund requests. Include role, rules, available tools, and tone.
the-system-prompt.placeholder: You are a customer service agent...
the-system-prompt.hint: You are a customer service agent for ShopEase. Rules: approve refunds under $50 auto, escalate over $50. Tools: order_lookup, refund_process, escalate_to_human. Tone: empathetic, professional.
the-system-prompt.successMessage: A great system prompt is an agent's DNA.

tool-calling-basics.instruction: Define a tool for an AI agent in plain language. Include: name, description, required parameters, and what it returns.
tool-calling-basics.successMessage: You just wrote a tool definition. This is exactly how you give an agent new capabilities.

tools-and-capabilities.instruction: Design a toolkit (3-5 tools) for an AI agent that manages social media. Name each tool and explain when the agent would use it.
tools-and-capabilities.successMessage: A well-designed toolkit separates a useful agent from a toy demo.

memory-matters.instruction: Design a memory system for a personal assistant agent. Describe short-term vs long-term memory, storage, and recall triggers.
memory-matters.successMessage: Memory is what makes an agent feel like it truly knows you.

error-handling.instruction: Your agent calls a tool and it returns an error. Write 3 recovery strategies it should try, in order, before giving up.
error-handling.successMessage: Graceful error handling makes agents production-ready.

design-your-agent.instruction: Design your own AI agent: purpose, system prompt summary, 3-5 tools, memory approach, and what it escalates to a human.
design-your-agent.successMessage: You just designed a complete AI agent. This is a real spec you could build from.

evaluating-your-agent.instruction: Write 3 test cases for evaluating a customer service agent. Each: input, expected behavior, pass/fail criteria.
evaluating-your-agent.successMessage: Testing agents is harder than testing code — but you just wrote real eval criteria.

agent-quiz.instruction: Pitch your AI agent to a skeptical CTO in 3 sentences. Problem it solves, how it works, ROI.
agent-quiz.successMessage: If you can pitch it in 3 sentences, you understand it deeply enough to build it.
